page: Google Cloud Platform m3-megamem-128 is a memory-optimized server instance configured with 128 vCPUs and 1952 GB of RAM. Powered by an Intel Xeon processor running at 2.6 GHz, the architecture features 64 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, resulting in 2 threads per core. The CPU includes a 54 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and cryptography acceleration via AES and SHA_NI. This instance does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, relying entirely on network-attached storage for persistent data.

In benchmark evaluations, the m3-megamem-128 demonstrates strong multi-core performance and memory throughput. The stress-ng multi-core CPU test yielded a strong score of 120,736.75, while the Geekbench multi-core score reached 17,468.0. Memory bandwidth is a key strength, with read-only small block cached bandwidth measured at 6,507.69 GB/sec and large block uncached read bandwidth at 298.49 GB/sec. Database operations are highly efficient, as evidenced by a strong Passmark database score of 28,527.39 and a Redis SET throughput of 106.54 million operations per second. However, single-core performance remains in the average tier, with a Geekbench single-core score of 1,578.0.

The high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 15.25 GB per core positions this instance as a specialized resource for memory-intensive workloads rather than general compute tasks. From a cost-efficiency perspective, the high density of RAM relative to CPU cores provides a consolidated platform for large-scale in-memory databases, enterprise resource planning systems, and heavy data-processing pipelines. A notable tradeoff is the lack of local NVMe storage and GPUs, which limits its utility for local high-speed scratch space or hardware-accelerated machine learning training. While LLM prompt processing for small models is strong at 2,958.91 tokens per second, large model text generation is weak at 2.73 tokens per second, confirming that heavy AI inference is better suited for GPU-accelerated instances.
description: Google Cloud Platform m3-megamem-128 is a memory-optimized server instance designed for memory-intensive enterprise workloads. It features 128 vCPUs and 1952 GB of RAM, powered by an Intel Xeon processor with 64 physical cores operating at 2.6 GHz. The hardware architecture includes a 54 MB L3 cache and supports AVX-512 and cryptographic acceleration. Benchmarks show strong multi-core capabilities, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 120,736.75, and high memory bandwidth, reaching 298.49 GB/sec for large block reads. Database performance is also strong, highlighted by a Redis SET throughput of 106.54 million operations per second. However, the instance lacks local storage and GPUs, and single-core performance is average. This configuration is best suited for large in-memory databases, data-processing pipelines, and memory-heavy enterprise applications.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform m3-megamem-128 is a memory-optimized instance with 128 vCPUs, 1952 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon processor, delivering strong multi-core and memory bandwidth performance for enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform m3-megamem-128 is a memory-optimized instance with 128 vCPUs and 1952 GB RAM, designed for large in-memory databases.
tagline: A memory-optimized architecture featuring massive RAM capacity and strong multi-core performance for memory-intensive enterprise database workloads.
bullet_points: 128 vCPUs and 1952 GB of RAM powered by Intel Xeon processors
Strong memory bandwidth reaching 298.49 GB/sec for large block reads
High database throughput with 106.54 million Redis SET operations per second
No local NVMe storage or dedicated GPU accelerators included
Best suited for large-scale in-memory databases and heavy data processing
